title: Tol dosLla

{{Quote|Imperial Star Destroyer ''Chimaera'', this is Tol dosLla of the Ukian Overliege. We would ask you to cease your bombardment of Ukio while we discuss terms for surrender.|Tol dosLla|<ref name="TLC" />}}
'''Tol dosLla''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]]. The eldest son of [[Gol dosLa]], the [[Ukian/Legends|Ukian]] [[Overliege]], dosLla served as the spokesman for the government. A capable and renowned [[Politics/Legends|politician]], dosLla was expected to succeed his [[Parent/Legends|father]]. In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] waged a [[Thrawn campaign|military campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Late in his campaign, Thrawn targeted Ukio, intent on capturing it and acquiring its food production and processing facilities for his [[Thrawn's fleet (Galactic Empire)|military forces]]. Following a [[Battle at Ukio|brief engagement]] against the planet—in which Thrawn effectively employed [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking technology]] to fool the Ukians into believing that he possessed a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] capable of firing through a [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]]—dosLla negotiated Ukio's surrender to Thrawn. Despite being overmatched, dosLla was able to successfully demand guarantees of safety for the Ukian people and their land during the Imperial occupation.

==Biography==
{{Quote|There will be no war-level tax levies? No forced conscription of our young people?|Tos dosLla, during negotiations with Grand Admiral Thrawn following the Battle of Ukio|<ref name="TLC" />}}
Tol dosLla was a [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="TLC1" /> [[Ukian/Legends|native]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]],<ref name="TLC" /> an [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural world]] in the [[Abrion sector/Legends|Abrion sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. DosLla was the eldest son of the Ukian [[Overliege]], [[Gol dosLa]], and by [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], he served as the spokesman for him. A capable [[Politics/Legends|politician]], dosLla was considered to be the next in line for the position of Overliege.<ref name="TLCSB" />

In 9 ABY, during the [[Thrawn campaign|military campaign]] propagated by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Ukio was one of the targets selected by Thrawn. The Grand Admiral wanted Ukio's extensive agricultural production and processing facilities to supply his [[Thrawn's fleet (Galactic Empire)|military forces]], and devised a strategy to render Ukio defenseless. Staging feints against a number of other worlds, Thrawn stretched the local New Republic forces thinly across the region. With the New Republic engaging Imperial forces in multiple [[Star system/Legends|systems]], Ukio was left defenseless. Leading a [[Task force/Legends|task force]] into the [[Ukio system/Legends|system]] headed by his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], Thrawn [[Battle at Ukio|demanded]] that Ukio surrender to the Empire.<ref name="TLC" />

Initially, the Ukians did not respond to Thrawn's ultimatum, issuing a distress call to the [[Ord Pardron sector defense outpost|New Republic base]] at [[Ord Pardron/Legends|Ord Pardron]], which provided for their local defense. Due to Thrawn's feints, however, Ord Pardron was unable to respond. Despite this, the Ukians felt secure, as they had a [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]] that surrounded their world. Thrawn expected this, and had already tasked a pair of [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] with civilian registries to insert several [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaked]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] below the planetary [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] by guiding them in under tow. Using careful positioning and calculated timing—along with the [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] of the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Joruus C'baoth]]—Thrawn created a ruse that made it appear that [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire from the ''Chimaera'' was penetrating the planetary shield and striking at targets on the surface. In reality, the ''Chimaera''{{'s}} fire was dissipating harmlessly on the shield, and the cloaked cruisers—positioned directly in line with the ''Chimaera''{{'s}} turbolaser fire—were firing the devastating shots from within the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]].<ref name="TLC" />

With the destruction of two Ukian military air bases, dosLla contacted Thrawn on behalf of the Overliege to request a cessation to the [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] and to discuss their terms of surrender. Thrawn, wishing to take the planet with its defenses intact, ordered dosLla to recall all military forces, lower the planetary shield and allow his forces to take control of the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] and all ground-to-[[Realspace/Legends|space]] weapons. Despite these changes, as long as the Ukian people behaved, Thrawn assured them that their societal and political structure would remain untouched. Suspicious, dosLla inquired about forced conscription and war-level taxation. Since Thrawn had attained cloning facilities on the planet [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]], he had no need for conscripts and assured dosLla that Ukio's young people would not be called upon. He also told dosLla that, instead of war-level taxation, Ukio's contribution to the war effort would come from supplying Imperial forces with food from their extensive agriculture industry.<ref name="TLC" />

Despite knowing that Thrawn's changes were the first steps in transforming Ukio into a supply depot for the Empire, dosLla knew that he had no alternative but to comply with the Grand Admiral's demands, since failure to do so would lead to the destruction of Ukio and its society. Defeated but defiant, dosLla informed Thrawn that they were lowering the planetary shields as a gesture of good faith, but before the shield generators and weapons were turned over, he wanted guarantees regarding the safety of the Ukian people under Imperial rule. Thrawn, knowing that allowing the Ukian people to surrender with dignity would stall the inevitable resistance to their control, informed dosLla that a representative would be dispatched to discuss terms from the Overliege. With no objections, dosLla ordered the shield lowered so that Imperial forces could take up preliminary defensive positions.<ref name="TLC" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|We will lower the planetary shields, ''Chimaera'', as a gesture of good faith. But before the generators and ground/space weaponry can be turned over to Imperial forces we shall require certain guarantees regarding the safety of the Ukian people and our land.|Tol dosLla, to Grand Admiral Thrawn, during Ukio's surrender to Imperial forces|<ref name="TLC" />}}
Well known as a capable and effective politician, Tol dosLla was respected across Ukio. He possessed an agreeable nature, and was very soft spoken. However, he was not afraid to speak his mind, and used his gentle nature to talk individuals around to his way of thinking; this was a strategy that dosLla used on several occasions during his career.<ref name="TLCSB" /> DosLla was chosen as the representative of the Overliege to negotiate Ukio's surrender to Imperial forces led by Grand Admiral Thrawn in 9 ABY. Despite facing overwhelming firepower—and the belief that Thrawn had somehow developed turbolaser capabilities that were able to fire through an intact planetary shield—dosLla was strong enough in his negotiations to demand assurances regarding the safety of the Ukian people and their land.<ref name="TLC" />

==Skills and abilities==
DosLla was knowledgeable about the Ukian bureaucracy, and had an understanding of other [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and languages. He was very knowledgeable about the business of agriculture, and was experienced in valuing the price of foodstuffs and obtaining the best price for them.<ref name="TLCSB" />

==Equipment==
DosLla always carried a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] that contained the harvest production schedules.<ref name="TLCSB" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ukian-AE.png|right|thumb|A canonical depiction of a Ukian from ''Alien Encounters'']]
Tol dosLla first appeared in ''[[The Last Command]]'', the third book of the [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''Thrawn Trilogy'']], written by [[Timothy Zahn]]. He subsequently appeared in the [[The Last Command 1|first issue]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] adaptation of the [[Star Wars: The Last Command (comic series)|''The Last Command'']]. DosLla also gained a small capsule entry in the ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'', published by [[West End Games]], which was reprinted in ''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''.

Despite Ukians being described in ''The Last Command Sourcebook'' as hairless, green-skinned and red-eyed, the depiction of dosLla in ''The Last Command 1'' portrayed him as a Human with light skin, brown hair and a beard. As other Humans, such as the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Janse]]<ref name="Endor-Janse">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Corporal Janse}}</ref> and the Imperial [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]] [[Oberk]],<ref name="Endor-Oberk">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Corporal Oberk}}</ref> are known to have been born on Ukio, this article assumes that dosLla is a Human of Ukian nationality.

DosLla's name has also been misspelled in various sources; both his first appearance in ''The Last Command'' and the comic issue ''The Last Command 1'' spelled his name as "Tol dosLla." However, the name was spelled as "dosLa," without the second "l," in ''The Last Command Sourcebook''. This spelling extended to the name of Gol dosLa, the character's father. ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2009]], used the double "l" spelling, effectively [[canon]]izing that name as the correct spelling. Since no mention was made of his father in the ''Encyclopedia'', his father still retains the dosLa surname.
